channel.title: Research & Articles on Toki Tahmid | Digital Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content in Research & Articles on Toki Tahmid | Digital Portfolio

item.title: Deconstructing the Cosmos: First-Principles Thinking in Aerospace Engineering
item.description: Why traditional analogies fail in deep space, and how first-principles reasoning is the only way to engineer the unknown.

item.title: Applying First Principles to Multidisciplinary Engineering
item.description:
The Fundamental Breakdown
#

In the pursuit of advanced aerospace systems, we often become entirely consumed by the mechanical hardware—thrust, drag, computational fluid dynamics, and material tensile strength. However, any system designed to carry or support human life must ultimately answer to the constraints of biology.

Through my independent studies into anatomy, physiology, and pathology, I have realized that the human body is the most complex mechanical system we will ever encounter.

item.title: The Biological Payload: Engineering for Human Endurance in Aerospace Systems
item.description: Exploring the critical intersection of mechanical engineering and biomedical science in the design of extra-terrestrial life-support systems.

item.title: Wireless Frontiers: The Engineering of Long-Distance Power Transmission
item.description: An analytical breakdown of Microwave Power Transmission (MPT) systems, focusing on magnetron efficiency and the Friis Transmission Equation.
